text.title: k6 and Locust Scripting Patterns
text.summary: Scripting patterns for k6 (JavaScript) and Locust (Python) with staged ramp configuration, threshold setup, and CI integration.

rule.statement: Always run a 30-second smoke test at 5 VUs before any real load test. If smoke fails, fix the script before touching the system under test.
rule.rationale: Agents that skip smoke tests waste 30 minutes of real load runs on a broken script. Smoke at 5 VUs confirms authentication works, scenarios parse, and all HTTP routes respond — the minimum correctness check.

section.title: k6 Patterns
section.reference: k6 scenario with staged ramp, thresholds, weighted scenario functions

rule.statement: In k6, always export raw JSON (--out json=results.json) for trend analysis; HTML reports are throwaway. Pin tool version in CI (grafana/k6:0.50.0) — minor versions change metric names.
rule.rationale: JSON output enables diffing runs across commits. Unpinned versions produce metric name mismatches that silently break threshold checks.

rule.statement: k6 is JavaScript but does not support Node.js APIs. Do not import fs, axios, or node built-ins — they don't exist in k6's runtime.
rule.rationale: Agents hallucinate k6 module names (k6/http2 doesn't exist). Run k6 inspect script.js before any real run to catch import errors.

section.p: Standard threshold pattern: http_req_duration p(95) < 500ms, http_req_failed rate < 1%, custom errorRate < 5%.
Standard stage pattern: 2m ramp to 50 VUs, 5m hold, 2m ramp to 100 VUs, 5m hold, 2m ramp down.

section.title: Locust Patterns
section.reference: Locust scenario with weighted tasks, auth setup, custom metrics

rule.statement: In Locust, always use self.client for HTTP calls — never use the requests library directly. Calls via requests are invisible to Locust's measurement layer.
rule.rationale: Invisible requests produce false-negative results: the scenario runs but Locust shows no requests in the dashboard or output.

section.p: Use @task(N) weight to model realistic user behavior (browse 10x more than checkout 1x).

rule.statement: Always include realistic think time in scenarios: sleep(random(1, 4)) in k6, between(1, 5) in Locust. Without think time, you benchmark the load generator, not the SUT.
rule.rationale: Real users don't fire requests as fast as possible. Scenarios without think time produce artificially high request rates that break before revealing actual capacity limits.

section.title: CI Integration
section.reference: GitHub Actions workflow: k6 gate + pytest benchmarks

section.title: Distributed Load
section.p: Local laptop runs are CPU-bound on the load generator before the SUT reaches capacity. Use distributed mode past ~500 RPS: k6 cloud (grafana/k6-cloud), Locust master/worker setup. Tag all runs with git SHA and scenario name in metric labels for trend analysis.
